topic: An artist's work is displayed without their permission
raw_output:
[story 1]

The gallery lights felt too bright, searing the edges of the canvas. I stood frozen just inside the doorway, my breath catching in my throat. My eyes darted from the vibrant, chaotic swirls of my latest series to the hushed, admiring crowd. Then, a sudden, sharp intake of air. A man in a crisp suit, whose face I vaguely recognized from an art critic’s column, was gesturing emphatically toward a piece that was undeniably mine. My hand instinctively tightened around the strap of my portfolio, the leather digging into my palm. A cold knot tightened in my stomach, pulling my shoulders up toward my ears. "Excuse me," I managed, my voice sounding thin and reedy in the sudden quiet of my panic. "That piece... it's mine. I didn't authorize this display."

[story 2]

I walked into the studio expecting the familiar scent of turpentine and linseed oil, but what hit me was the sterile smell of polished marble and expensive perfume. My heart began to hammer against my ribs, a frantic, uneven rhythm. On the pristine white wall, hanging center stage, was my portrait series—the very ones I had been agonizing over for months. A gallery attendant was smiling, nodding approvingly at a patron. I moved forward, my steps clumsy and heavy. "Where is the paperwork?" I demanded, my tone sharper than I intended, a tremor running through my hands. When the attendant merely offered a practiced, vacant smile, the blood drained from my face. The sheer audacity of it, the complete disregard for my wishes, made my vision swim slightly at the edges.

[story 3]

The gallery was packed, a sea of murmuring voices and clicking cameras. I was trying to navigate the throng, my focus narrowed entirely on the section where my work was hung. Then, I saw it—my signature piece, illuminated under a spotlight that felt accusatory. A woman approached, her expression one of intense, almost proprietary satisfaction as she circled the frame. I stopped dead in my tracks. A wave of heat washed over my skin, making my palms slick. I took a hesitant step forward, my gaze locked on her, trying to project authority I absolutely did not feel. "Stop," I said, the word emerging as a low, strained rasp. "You need to take that down. Immediately." The silence that followed was thick, heavy, and utterly suffocating.
